sections.heading: Page 2
paragraphs:
1. Executive Overview
What AI Chef is
GovSecure AI Chef™ is a branded operating model for SMB AI governance. It converts AI risk, control,
ownership, and assurance into a recipe system that busy operators can actually use.
Why SMBs need this
 SMBs are adopting AI faster than they are defining ownership, review paths, and acceptable uses.
 Most governance materials are either too abstract for operations or too enterprise-heavy for lean
teams.
 Paid buyers want something they can drop into a live program without needing a six-week translation
exercise.
Primary buyer profile
SMB owners, operators, and lean technology or compliance leaders who need AI guardrails without
building a giant bureaucracy.
What the paid edition adds
 Realistic sample data so teams understand what good looks like.
 Template pages with clear required fields, review cycles, and evidence expectations.
 Appendices that make the toolkit feel implementation-ready rather than decorative.
2. Five-step adoption flow
1 2 3 4 5
Identify Assess Assign Control Review
GovSecure AI Chef™ | Paid SMB Edition | v1.1 client-ready draft

sections.heading: Page 7
paragraphs:
5. AI Use Case Intake and Risk Tiering
Realistic sample intake
Use case: Customer support co-pilot that drafts responses, summarizes tickets, and suggests refund
options to human agents.
Field Example entry
Business purpose Reduce average ticket handling time by 20% while
keeping a human agent in the loop.
Users Support agents and team leads.
Data used Customer emails, order history, product knowledge
base, refund policy snippets.
External impact Customer-facing outputs may be sent after agent
approval.
Decision type Recommendations only; no autonomous refunds.
Regulatory relevance Privacy review required because customer data
and retention are involved.
Human oversight Mandatory agent approval before send or action.
Proposed risk tier Tier 2 - Moderate, with Tier 3 controls if refund
automation is later enabled.
Risk tier logic
 Tier 1 - Low: internal productivity, low sensitivity, no external decision effect.
 Tier 2 - Moderate: business process support, limited customer or employee impact, strong human
review.
 Tier 3 - High: meaningful operational, legal, HR, financial, or customer impact; enhanced review
required.
 Tier 4 - Prohibited / Executive review: disallowed use or activity requiring executive decision and
documented exception.
GovSecure AI Chef™ | Paid SMB Edition | v1.1 client-ready draft

Registry rule of thumb
If a team can name the AI tool but cannot name the owner, review cycle, or permitted use, the registry
entry is not complete. That gap is usually where risk starts breeding.
GovSecure AI Chef™ | Paid SMB Edition | v1.1 client-ready draft

sections.heading: Page 10
paragraphs:
8. Monitoring, Logging, and AI Incident Response
 Monitor drift, misuse, access changes, exceptions, and failed or unusual automated actions.
 Review logs on a defined cadence rather than waiting for the fancy disaster to announce itself.
 Treat harmful outputs, privacy leaks, and connector misuse as operational incidents with owners and
evidence.
Recommended incident categories
 Harmful output
 Privacy breach
 Security exposure
 Model or prompt drift
 Unauthorized automation
 Third-party service failure
Reusable page structure preview
GovSecure AI Chef™ | Paid SMB Edition | v1.1 client-ready draft

sections.heading: Page 13
paragraphs:
Appendix A. Governance Glossary
AI system: Any tool, model, workflow, or agent used to generate outputs, recommendations, or actions.
Agent: An AI-enabled workflow that can take or suggest actions using tools, connectors, or instructions.
High-impact use case: A use case with meaningful financial, legal, HR, customer, or safety impact.
Human oversight: A defined review or approval step performed by an accountable human.
Model drift: A change in model behavior or performance over time that affects output quality or
safety.
Shadow AI: AI usage outside approved tools, workflows, or review paths.
Knowledge base: The source material or repository used to ground AI responses.
Prohibited use: A disallowed use case or activity that requires an exception to proceed.
Appendix B. Standards alignment crosswalk

planning themes

decisions

response improvement cybersecurity
